Re: Livermore-Amador Valley Transit Authority/Chanber

Dear Mr. Rothman:
This office represents the Livermore-Amador Valley Transit
Authority, 87 Fenton Street, Suite 200, Livermore, California
94550.
On April L9, 1990, the Livermore Chamber of Commerce amended
its By-Laws to include the following provision:
"Article I. 
Secti-on 4. LIMITATIONS OF METHODS. The
association shall be nonprofit, nonpartisan and non-
section. The association may endorse issues on an
official 
ballot, or candidates for elective office by
a two-thirds vote of a quorum of the board. Said vote
shaLl be based on criteria for endorsement established
by the board. The association shall not by resolution
or otherwise be committed to the financial support of,
nor may it engaqe in the active campaign of any candi-
date for elective offi-ce. Menbership in the associa-
tion is open to all without regard to race, coIor,
creed, alre or sex. "
This provision of the by-laws limits the Chamber's invol-vement in
political affairs to the endorsement of candidates and issues. tle
have discussed with officials of the Chamber the form that these
endorsements would take. Their response was that the endorsements
would be included in the Chamber's reqrularly published newsletter
in a fashion similar to that of a newspaper editorial.

sections.question_synthetic: Can the Livermore-Amador Valley Transit Authority maintain membership in the Livermore Chamber of Commerce given the Chamber's amended by-laws permitting political endorsements, and can membership fees be kept separate through bookkeeping to avoid violations?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The legal question is embedded in the body of the letter and a reference to prior telephone advice is made. Document lacks the typical FPPC advice letter structure.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can the Livermore-Amador Valley Transit Authority maintain membership in the Livermore Chamber of Commerce given the Chamber's amended by-laws permitting political endorsements, and can membership fees be kept separate through bookkeeping to avoid violations?

embedding.summary: Attorney Keith Fraser requests written FPPC advice on behalf of the Livermore-Amador Valley Transit Authority regarding the propriety of the Transit Authority's membership in the Livermore Chamber of Commerce following the Chamber's amendment of its by-laws to permit political endorsements of candidates and ballot issues.
